definition: The central bank reaction function — specifically the Taylor Rule inflation coefficient (phi_pi) — is the key institutional determinant of the stock-bond correlation regime. Historical calibration shows Burns-Miller Fed (phi_pi 0.8-1.0, violating Taylor Principle) produced positive correlation; Greenspan-Bernanke (phi_pi 1.5-2.0) produced negative correlation. Cross-country CB independence index correlates strongly with correlation sign.
evidence: Burns-Miller Fed (phi_pi 0.8-1.0) → positive correlation; Greenspan-Bernanke (phi_pi 1.5-2.0) → negative correlation
CB independence index across 20+ countries: Switzerland (0.92 independence → -0.35 correlation) vs Turkey (0.25 → +0.50 to +0.70)
Powell Fed 525bp hiking cycle demonstrated credibility (phi_pi approximately 1.5-2.0)
Five agents independently identified CB credibility as the critical mediating variable
open_questions: What is the current Powell Fed reaction function? 60% probability Fed maintains Taylor Rule adherence, 30% somewhat compromised, 10% fundamentally impaired
How will political pressure on Fed independence and fiscal dominance dynamics affect the reaction function?
Can central banks unilaterally restore negative correlation, or are structural factors beyond their control?
